title: Mermaid diagram renderer with JSDOM compatibility fixes
purpose: Renders Mermaid diagrams as SVG with fixes for JSDOM's limitations in calculating text bounds and element positions. Provides thumbnail generation with caching.
module_context: Exports a Renderer class that manages diagram rendering and caching of thumbnails. Includes utility functions for fixing SVG dimensions and viewBox in JSDOM environments.

steps.content: Identify node groups in the SVG that contain rectangles and foreignObjects with text content.
Calculate appropriate dimensions for each node based on the length of its text content.
Update the dimensions of both the rectangle and foreignObject elements to match the calculated size.
Locate edge label foreignObjects that have zero width and height.
Calculate proper dimensions for each edge label based on its text content.
Update the dimensions and positioning of the foreignObjects to display the labels correctly.
Extract transform attributes and element positions from the SVG to determine overall bounds.
Parse rectangle and circle elements to calculate minimum and maximum coordinates.
Calculate a new viewBox that encompasses all elements with appropriate padding.
Update the viewBox and max-width style attributes of the SVG to reflect the calculated bounds.
Initialize the mermaid library with the specified theme and disable automatic loading.
Generate the SVG diagram from the provided mermaid content.
Apply fixes to node dimensions to correct JSDOM rendering issues.
Apply fixes to edge labels to correct their display dimensions.
Apply fixes to the viewBox to properly size the diagram for JSDOM environment.
Return the fully corrected SVG string.
Check if a cached thumbnail exists for the given ID and return it if available.
Generate the SVG diagram for the provided content using the default theme.
Convert the SVG string to a UTF-8 buffer.
Implement cache management by removing the oldest entry if the cache exceeds 100 items.
Store the generated buffer in the cache under the provided ID.
Return the generated thumbnail buffer.
Remove all entries from the thumbnail cache.
